articles.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5 with Native Multimodal Reasoning and Extended Autonomous Task Execution
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5, featuring deeply integrated multimodal reasoning across text, image, audio, and video inputs with significantly improved long-horizon planning. The model demonstrates near-human performance on several graduate-level STEM benchmarks and introduces a persistent agent mode capable of autonomously executing multi-step workflows over hours. Industry analysts note the leap in agentic reliability as a potential inflection point for enterprise AI adoption.

articles.title: Google DeepMind's Gemini 2.5 Ultra Achieves State-of-the-Art on Scientific Discovery Benchmarks
articles.summary: DeepMind unveiled Gemini 2.5 Ultra, which sets new records on MathOdyssey and SciCode benchmarks by combining chain-of-thought reasoning with a retrieval-augmented architecture over curated scientific corpora. The model has already been deployed internally at Google to accelerate materials science research, reportedly reducing candidate screening time for novel battery cathode materials by 60%. The release intensifies the frontier model race between Google, OpenAI, and Anthropic.

articles.title: Figure Robotics Demonstrates Full Warehouse Autonomy with Figure 03 Humanoid Fleet
articles.summary: Figure Robotics showcased a fleet of its third-generation humanoid robots operating autonomously in a BMW logistics facility, handling palletizing, inventory scanning, and inter-zone transport without human intervention for an entire 8-hour shift. The robots use on-device vision-language models for real-time task adaptation and natural-language instruction following from supervisors. The demonstration marks a significant milestone in commercial humanoid deployment at scale.

articles.title: Boston Dynamics and Toyota Research Institute Unveil Dexterous Bi-Manual Manipulation Platform
articles.summary: A joint project between Boston Dynamics and the Toyota Research Institute produced a new robotic manipulation system that combines Atlas-derived hardware with TRI's diffusion-policy-based learning framework. The platform demonstrates human-level dexterity in tasks such as cable routing, small-parts assembly, and tool use, trained primarily in simulation with minimal real-world fine-tuning. The partnership signals a broader industry convergence between legacy robotics firms and foundation-model-driven control approaches.

articles.title: Anthropic Introduces Constitutional Classifiers for Real-Time AI Safety Monitoring in Production Systems
articles.summary: Anthropic released a new open-source safety framework called Constitutional Classifiers, designed to detect harmful, biased, or policy-violating outputs from any LLM in real time with sub-10ms latency overhead. The system uses lightweight classifier heads distilled from Claude's internal alignment representations and can be deployed as a sidecar to existing inference pipelines. Early adopters in financial services and healthcare report a 90% reduction in flagged outputs without meaningful impact on response quality.

articles.title: TSMC Begins Volume Production of A14 2nm Node with Backside Power Delivery for AI Accelerators
articles.summary: TSMC confirmed volume production on its N2 process node featuring backside power delivery network (BSPDN) technology, enabling a 15% performance uplift and 30% power reduction compared to its 3nm node. The first customers include Apple and NVIDIA, with the latter expected to use the node for its next-generation Blackwell Ultra AI GPU architecture. The ramp is on schedule and represents the most significant transistor-level architectural change in TSMC's manufacturing history.

articles.title: Meta Open-Sources Full Llama 4 Maverick Model Weights and Training Infrastructure Code
articles.summary: Meta released the complete weights, training code, and data pipeline tooling for Llama 4 Maverick, its 400B-parameter mixture-of-experts model, under the permissive Llama Community License. The release includes the FSDP2 and context-parallelism infrastructure that enabled training on a 48,000 H100 cluster, giving the open-source community unprecedented access to frontier-scale training methodology. Researchers have already begun fine-tuning domain-specific variants for legal reasoning and biomedical literature synthesis.

articles.title: Sanctuary AI and Magna International Partner to Deploy Humanoid Robots on Automotive Assembly Lines
articles.summary: Canadian robotics company Sanctuary AI announced a partnership with Magna International to integrate its Phoenix humanoid robots into automotive parts assembly workflows, starting with door panel and wiring harness installation. The robots leverage Sanctuary's Carbon AI system, which uses a world model trained on millions of hours of manipulation data to generalize across novel assembly tasks with minimal reprogramming. The deal includes deployment at three Magna facilities in Ontario by Q3 2026, representing one of the largest humanoid-in-manufacturing contracts announced to date.
